questions.text: An object has a negative charge. How must it have gotten this charge?

answerChoices.text: It gained electrons from another object.
It lost electrons to another object.
It gained protons from another object.
It lost protons to another object.

questions.text: An object has a positive charge. How must it have gotten this charge?

answerChoices.text: It gained electrons from another object.
It lost electrons to another object.
It gained protons from another object.
It lost protons to another object.

questions.text: An object is considered neutral if:

answerChoices.text: It has the same number as positive and negative charges.

questions.text: An object is considered polarized if...

answerChoices.text: Its net charge is neutral, but one end has more positive charge and the other end is more negatively charged.

questions.text: In the typical image of an atom, there are electrons surrounding protons and neutrons. Why are there electrons?

answerChoices.text: They are attracted to the protons in the nucleus

questions.text: In the macroscopic world (the real world), how can you typically determine an object is charged?

answerChoices.text: It will be tingly to the touch.
